title: RESOLVED/CLOSED 2026-07-14: both documented triggers already handled; the proposed terminal-bridge-error degrade is WRONG (it breaks the intentional non-infra-stays-cycle-fatal integrity invariant). Kept only as a low-weight note in case a specific infra exit code (124/127) is found reaching the terminal abort for an OPTIONAL non-floor phase.
summary: INVESTIGATED + LARGELY CLOSED 2026-07-14 (operator, branch concurrency-verdict-surface-s2, reverted). The item's premise was a MISDIAGNOSIS: cycle-823's LIVE PROOF ('coverage-gate WARN discards cycle') was traced to the recorded verdicts — coverage-gate-usage.json = PASS, audit-usage.json = FAIL — i.e. it was the AUDIT-VERDICT CLOBBER, now fixed by session-lifecycle-verdict-clobbers-ondisk-deliverable (settle-retry, commit c2258b72). The item's ORIGINAL trigger (cycle-742 test-amplification `[bad_verdict]` after 2 corrections) is ALREADY handled by the landed non-canonical-verdict degrade at cyclerun_dispatch.go (contract_exhaustion_skip via nonFloorExhaustionDegrade + recordFinalVerdict; test TestContractExhaustion_NonFloorPhase_DegradesToSkippedWarn). So BOTH documented triggers are covered. I built + tested a sibling degrade for the TERMINAL-BRIDGE-ERROR abort site (mirror of :375) — it PASSED its own wiring-proof test but REGRESSED TestOptionalPhaseNonInfraErrorStaysCycleFatal, revealing a DELIBERATE integrity invariant: an optional non-floor phase failing with a NON-infra error (e.g. amplify-tests 'forged challenge token' — a gaming/logic failure) MUST stay cycle-fatal; only infra-shaped weather (transient/timeout/quota) may be skipped. A blanket non-floor terminal-error degrade would SWALLOW real integrity/gaming failures — so it was reverted. The infra-error cases that SHOULD degrade are already covered: optionalInfraSkip (orchestrator.go:58) degrades Optional non-floor phases on transient/ErrArtifactTimeout; allFamiliesQuotaExhausted checkpoints+defers all-family exit=85. The only case a broad degrade would newly help — a NON-Optional non-floor post-floor phase — appears EMPTY in practice (minted phases are forced Optional; built-in post-floor phases memo/retro are handled by postShipObserverSkip / recordFailureLearning). RESIDUAL (why this survives at 0.25, INVESTIGATION not defect): verify whether any specific infra exit code (80 boot / 124 gnu-timeout / 127 not-found) is NOT classified by isTransientBridgeError and could therefore reach the terminal abort for an OPTIONAL non-floor phase that should have infra-skipped. If found, the correct fix is to EXTEND isTransientBridgeError / optionalInfraSkip's error classification for those codes — NOT a blanket non-floor degrade. resolveShipProceed (cycle-831 orphan) is NOT needed; do not resurrect it.
acceptance: INVESTIGATION: audit isTransientBridgeError coverage against the real bridge exit vocabulary (80/81/85/124/127); list which reach the terminal abort for an OPTIONAL non-floor post-floor phase

IF a gap is found: extend optionalInfraSkip's infra-error classification (NOT a blanket degrade) so the specific infra code degrades for optional non-floor phases; keep TestOptionalPhaseNonInfraErrorStaysCycleFatal green (non-infra stays cycle-fatal)
IF no gap: close this item
source: operator investigation 2026-07-14: reverted broad terminal-bridge-error degrade (regressed TestOptionalPhaseNonInfraErrorStaysCycleFatal); both documented triggers already handled (fix #1 + existing contract_exhaustion_skip).
